user_query:
Set up a one-week sprint backlog in our local task manager using the provided files and produce reproducible reports.

Instructions:
1) Read input/backlog.csv and add every row as a new task in the local task manager with these fields:
   - title (required)
   - priority (P0/P1/P2/P3; default to P2 if missing)
   - tags (comma-separated)
   - due (string date)
   - desc (description)
   All newly created tasks should start in status "pending".

2) After creating all tasks, apply the operations in input/updates.json in order. Operations can include:
   - start: set status to in_progress for the matching task title
   - complete: set status to completed for the matching task title
   - archive: set status to archived for the matching task title
   - update_priority: change priority for the matching task title
   - update_due: change due date for the matching task title
   - delete: remove the task entirely
   The file will also include a "due_by" date used for a filtered view (see step 4).

3) Write these outputs using only relative paths:
   - output/created_tasks.json: an array of objects for all tasks immediately after creation and before updates are applied. Each object must include: {"title", "id", "priority", "status", "tags" (array), "due"}. Sort this file by priority order P0, P1, P2, P3, and then by due date ascending (null/empty due last).
   - output/final_state.json: an array of objects for all tasks remaining after updates are applied (exclude any deleted tasks), with the same fields as above reflecting final values. Use the same sorting rule.
   - output/stats.txt: a plain-text summary with exactly these lines (one per line):
       Total: <N>
       Pending: <n_pending> | In Progress: <n_in_progress> | Completed: <n_completed> | Archived: <n_archived>
       P0: <n_p0> | P1: <n_p1> | P2: <n_p2> | P3: <n_p3>
       Completion rate: <rate>%
     where Completion rate is (completed/total * 100) rounded to one decimal place.
   - output/p0_p1_due_by.md: a short markdown list of all tasks that are priority P0 or P1 with a due date on or before the "due_by" date from input/updates.json, using a bullet list with each line "- <title> (due: <due>)". If none match, write "- None".

Notes:
- Use only the workspace-local task manager (no external services). Keep paths relative.
- Titles in the CSV are unique and should be preserved exactly.
- Make sure IDs in created_tasks.json and final_state.json are the same for the same task.
- Do not include any other files besides the four outputs listed.
